text.title: AI Agent Prompt Handover Core Rules
text.summary: Four testable rules every application of AI Agent Prompt Handover enforces. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every AI Agent Prompt Handover prompt is versioned in source control (file path + git SHA) — not edited in a chat tab and lost.

rationale.source: prompt engineering / Anthropic Prompt Library best practices

rule.rationale: Untracked prompts produce unreproducible behavior. Bisecting a regression is impossible without history.

rule.statement: Prompts ship with an eval harness: ≥10 golden test cases run on every change. No eval-less prompts in production.

rationale.source: Prompt eval research / Schuster et al., 2023

rule.rationale: Eval-less prompts regress silently. The first user-reported issue is the first sign of breakage.

rule.statement: Cost and latency budget per call are declared in the prompt's metadata. Calls exceeding the budget are rejected.

rationale.source: FinOps for LLMs / OpenAI cost optimization guide

rule.rationale: Unbounded prompt cost gets discovered at end-of-month billing. Pre-declared budget catches at integration time.

rule.statement: Output schema is explicit (JSON schema, Pydantic, or grammar). Free-text outputs are forbidden where downstream parses.

rationale.source: structured output / Anthropic tool use, OpenAI function calling docs

rule.rationale: Free-text outputs require fragile regex parsers. Schema-validated outputs fail fast on drift.

example.title: Versioned prompt with eval reference
example.code: Prompt: prompts/jtbd-synth.txt@a3f1c Model: claude-opus-4.6 Budget: 4k tokens in, 1k out, $0.024 per call Eval: tests/jtbd-synth.spec.ts (10 golden cases)

example.title: Chat-tab prompt
example.code: (Pasted into Claude chat, no version history)
